title: Thailand with Kids — The Honest Family Guide (2026)
meta_desc: Thailand family holiday guide: best destinations, real costs, when to go, flight times from NZ, and which parts of Thailand are actually great with kids.
tagline: Warm, cheap, stunning — but you need to know where to go with kids
hero_alt: Krabi limestone cliffs and turquoise water, Thailand
flight_from_nz: 11–12 hours (Auckland to Bangkok, usually via Sydney or Singapore)
intro: Thailand is one of the best-value family destinations in the world — warm all year, incredible food, friendly locals, and a fraction of NZ prices. A family of 4 can live extremely well for NZ$200–250/day including a beach resort, food, and daily activities. The key is knowing which parts of Thailand suit families with kids.
verdict: Excellent for families, especially with kids aged 5+. Younger kids do well too if you pick the right destinations and go in the dry season.

best_destinations.name: Chiang Mai
best_destinations.tagline: Temples, elephants, and cool mountain air — the best family city in Thailand
best_destinations.why: Chiang Mai has the best ethical elephant experiences in Asia, fantastic street food, and a charming old city. Cooler than Bangkok, much less frantic, and very child-friendly. A week here is better than a week in Bangkok for most families.
must_do: Elephant Nature Park (ethical, book in advance)
Old City temples (Doi Suthep is the most stunning)
Doi Inthanon National Park (highest point in Thailand)
best_destinations.cost: Budget NZ$130–200/day family of 4 including accommodation

best_destinations.name: Krabi / Ao Nang
best_destinations.tagline: Thailand's most spectacular beaches — dramatic limestone cliffs, calm water
best_destinations.why: Krabi is more relaxed and less crowded than Phuket, with better beaches. The four islands boat trip ($40/person) is unmissable. Railay Beach (boat access only) is one of the world's most beautiful beaches.
must_do: Railay Beach day trip by longtail boat
Tiger Cave Temple (1,237 steps — reward kids at the top)
Rock climbing intro for older kids at Railay

best_destinations.name: Koh Lanta
best_destinations.tagline: Quieter, cheaper, and better for younger kids than Phuket or Koh Samui
best_destinations.why: Koh Lanta is what Koh Samui used to be — relaxed, beautiful, with long sandy beaches and calm water on the west coast. Far fewer party tourists than other Thai islands. Perfect for families with under-10s.
must_do: Long Beach (Hat Khlong Dao) — safest swimming beach on the island
Snorkelling day trip to Koh Rok

practical_info.currency: Thai Baht (THB). NZ$1 ≈ 22 THB (2026). Always use ATMs, not airport exchange.
practical_info.health: Get hep A, typhoid vaccinations before you go. Mosquito repellent essential (dengue risk). Drink bottled water only.
practical_info.food_safety: Cook-to-order street food is generally safer than tourist buffets. Pad thai, mango sticky rice, and khao man gai are reliable, delicious, and $2–4 per serve.
practical_info.sim_card: Buy a DTAC or AIS tourist SIM at Bangkok airport (100GB for NZ$25). Works countrywide.
practical_info.visa: NZ passport holders get 60-day visa-free entry as of 2025.
practical_info.transport: Domestic flights on Thai Lion Air or AirAsia are cheap (NZ$20–60 per person). Book well ahead.

budget_breakdown.accommodation: NZ$60–180/night for a good family room (Thailand has incredible value)
budget_breakdown.food: NZ$30–60/day eating local and street food. Double if eating at beach restaurants.
budget_breakdown.activities: NZ$40–120/day — most activities are half NZ prices
budget_breakdown.transport: NZ$20–50/day including tuk-tuks, taxis, and local boats

facebook_groups.name: Thailand with Kids

nz_affiliate_note: Book flights from NZ via Air New Zealand (Auckland–Bangkok direct via code-share) or Jetstar/Air Asia connecting through Singapore or Sydney.
